perturbation.original_text: 5.1.3. not copy or reproduce (or permit to be copied or reproduced) any Confidential Information, or directly or indirectly disclose or distribute any of it to any person other than those of its Representatives who are strictly required to perform Recipientȇs work in relation to the Project.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1.3. not directly or indirectly disclose or distribute any Confidential Information to any person other than those of its Representatives who are strictly required to perform Recipientȇs work in relation to the Project.
perturbation.explanation: The clause "not copy or reproduce (or permit to be copied or reproduced) any Confidential Information" has been removed. This creates an in-text contradiction as section 8.1.1 still instructs the 'Recipient' to return 'all documentation and other materials containing any Confidential Information of Discloser without retaining any copies thereof' and therefore contradicting the clause 5.1.3 in its new state.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.1.1. Upon Discloserȇs request, Recipient shall promptly, and in any case within five (5) days from such request, return all documentation and other materials containing any Confidential Information of Discloser without retaining any copies thereof. Alternatively, at Discloserȇs option and request, Recipient shall destroy and/or erase all such materials and documentation and shall provide a written certification that all such materials and documentation have been destroyed and/or erased.
perturbation.justification: The original text prevented the recipient from copying confidential information. The changed text removes this restriction, while section 8.1.1 still requires the recipient to return all documentation without retaining any copies. This creates a direct contradiction: the recipient is no longer explicitly prohibited from making copies under 5.1.3, but is still required to return all materials without retaining copies under 8.1.1, making it impossible to comply with both sections if copies are made. Therefore, the changed text strongly contradicts the requirement in section 8.1.1.

perturbation.original_text: 7.1.1. The obligations contained in this Agreement shall start at the Effective Date and shall automatically terminate when the Project is terminated or completed.
perturbation.changed_text: 7.1.1. The obligations contained in this Agreement shall start at the Effective Date.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "and shall automatically terminate when the Project is terminated or completed," the agreement's end date becomes ambiguous. This is an in-text contradiction with section 7.3.1, which still speaks about 'termination or expiration of this Confidentiality Agreement' as if there were defined terms for it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.3.1. The obligations of confidentiality, non-disclosure and non-use set forth in this Confidentiality Agreement shall survive the termination or expiration of this Confidentiality Agreement (i) with respect to any Confidential Information that constitutes a trade secret under applicable law, for so long as such item shall continue to constitute a trade secret under applicable law, and (ii) with respect to any Confidential Information that does not constitute a trade secret under applicable law, for a period of 5 (five) years from and after the date of disclosure of such Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The removal of 'and shall automatically terminate when the Project is terminated or completed' from section 7.1.1 creates an ambiguity regarding the agreement's end date. This contradicts section 7.3.1, which refers to the 'termination or expiration of this Confidentiality Agreement', implying a defined termination process that is no longer explicitly stated in 7.1.1 after the modification, thus causing a strong, clear contradiction. The omission of a termination clause where one is expected based on the rest of the document creates a direct conflict, therefore falling under 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 7.2.1. Each Party may terminate the Agreement by giving one month prior written notice to the other Party.
perturbation.explanation: Removing this clause means the agreement has no explicit termination clause. This omission introduces a contradiction with clause 7.3, which refers to 'termination or expiration of this Confidentiality Agreement'. Without 7.2.1, the conditions for legal termination are unclear, creating an in-text contradiction with section 7.3.1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.3.1. The obligations of confidentiality, non-disclosure and non-use set forth in this Confidentiality Agreement shall survive the termination or expiration of this Confidentiality Agreement (i) with respect to any Confidential Information that constitutes a trade secret under applicable law, for so long as such item shall continue to constitute a trade secret under applicable law, and (ii) with respect to any Confidential Information that does not constitute a trade secret under applicable law, for a period of 5 (five) years from and after the date of disclosure of such Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text (7.2.1) provided a method for termination of the agreement. The changed text removes this entire clause, creating a situation where the conditions for legal termination are unclear or nonexistent. The contradicted text (7.3.1) refers to 'termination or expiration' implying a mechanism for ending the agreement. Because the changed text eliminates the original explicit termination method, this conflicts with 7.3.1, creating an in-text contradiction because 7.3.1's survival clause is dependent on termination, and now the means of termination are unclear. The omission leads to uncertainty regarding how or if the agreement can be terminated and is a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8.1.2. Clause 8.1 shall not apply to Confidential Information which i) must be stored by Recipient according to provisions of mandatory law or ii) was made as a matter of routine backup provided that such Confidential Information and copies thereof shall be subject to an indefinite confidential obligation according to the terms and conditions set forth herein until returned and/or destroyed, as the case may be.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.1.2. Clause 8.1 shall not apply to Confidential Information which i) must be stored by Recipient according to provisions of mandatory law
perturbation.explanation: By removing the second exemption from 8.1 'or ii) was made as a matter of routine backup provided that such Confidential Information and copies thereof shall be subject to an indefinite confidential obligation according to the terms and conditions set forth herein until returned and/or destroyed, as the case may be', it creates a contradiction. If backups aren't an exemption, then 8.1 requires all backups of confidential information to be returned, even though 7.3.1 specifies different temporal validity depending of it constituting or not a trade secret. This contradicts because some backups might exist for more than 5 years.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.3.1. The obligations of confidentiality, non-disclosure and non-use set forth in this Confidentiality Agreement shall survive the termination or expiration of this Confidentiality Agreement (i) with respect to any Confidential Information that constitutes a trade secret under applicable law, for so long as such item shall continue to constitute a trade secret under applicable law, and (ii) with respect to any Confidential Information that does not constitute a trade secret under applicable law, for a period of 5 (five) years from and after the date of disclosure of such Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text included an exception in clause 8.1.2 for confidential information made as routine backups, subjecting them to an indefinite confidentiality obligation until returned or destroyed. This aligns with section 7.3.1, which specifies different confidentiality periods based on whether the information constitutes a trade secret (indefinite) or not (5 years). By removing the backup exception in the changed text, all backups are now subject to clause 8.1, which potentially requires their return. This contradicts 7.3.1 because backups that are not trade secrets should only be confidential for 5 years, but the modified 8.1 now implies they must be returned regardless, creating a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: 10.2.2. Any dispute, controversy or claim arising out of or in connection with the Agreement or any breach, termination or invalidity hereof which is not resolved by prior negotiations, shall be finally settled by arbitration by The Danish Institute of Arbitration in accordance with the relevant rules adapted by The Danish Institute of Arbitration and applicable at the time of opening of the arbitration. The arbitration tribunal must have three arbitrators appointed by the Danish Institute of Arbitration unless the Parties agree otherwise. The arbitrators elect, from among their members, the chairman of the arbitration tribunal.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.2.2. Any dispute, controversy or claim arising out of or in connection with the Agreement or any breach or invalidity hereof which is not resolved by prior negotiations, shall be finally settled by arbitration by The Danish Institute of Arbitration in accordance with the relevant rules adapted by The Danish Institute of Arbitration and applicable at the time of opening of the arbitration. The arbitration tribunal must have three arbitrators appointed by the Danish Institute of Arbitration unless the Parties agree otherwise. The arbitrators elect, from among their members, the chairman of the arbitration tribunal.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the word 'termination' from the sentence 'Any dispute, controversy or claim arising out of or in connection with the Agreement or any breach, termination or invalidity hereof', it suggests that disputes related to termination are not subject to arbitration. This is an in-text contradiction, as section 7.2.1 (now removed in a previous perturbation, making the contract more contradictory) provided for termination, and this section now creates ambiguity about how termination disputes should be resolved, while earlier ones assume it exists and it's arbitratable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.2.2. Any dispute, controversy or claim arising out of or in connection with the Agreement or any breach or invalidity hereof which is not resolved by prior negotiations, shall be finally settled by arbitration by The Danish Institute of Arbitration in accordance with the relevant rules adapted by The Danish Institute of Arbitration and applicable at the time of opening of the arbitration. The arbitration tribunal must have three arbitrators appointed by the Danish Institute of Arbitration unless the Parties agree otherwise. The arbitrators elect, from among their members, the chairman of the arbitration tribunal.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'termination' from the list of disputes subject to arbitration, while the contradicted text implicitly assumes that termination disputes are handled similarly to disputes arising from breach or invalidity. This omission creates a contradiction, as it's now unclear if termination disputes are subject to arbitration, making enforcement uncertain and creating a logical inconsistency.
